expectations.text: Identifies the work as belonging to the Deployment & Domains area of the platform
expectations.evidence: Line 5: names the work as spanning Backend Services, Deployment & Domains, and Celery & Background Workers.

expectations.text: Recommends modeling the flow as a resumable state machine that commits each transition (Principle 16), not one blocking function
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Principle 16' section (lines 9-15) and line 34: 'Replace the one blocking finalize_domain ... with a small resumable state machine.'

expectations.text: Recommends the domain move through explicit persisted intermediate states that can resume after a crash
expectations.evidence: Step 1 (line 36): define explicit states dns_verified -> gsc_pending -> gsc_verified -> active, each transition committed so a crash leaves recoverable state.

expectations.text: Recommends running the slow external GSC verification work asynchronously off the request path (Principle 19)
expectations.evidence: 'Principle 19' section (lines 17-19) and step 3 (line 45): GSC poll runs as a Celery task; route returns immediately; client polls.

expectations.text: Recommends bounding the verification retries with backoff and an explicit terminal state (Principle 21)
expectations.evidence: 'Principle 21' section (lines 21-23): finite, backed-off retry terminating in retry_exhausted.

expectations.text: Explicitly advises against the user's proposed single blocking finalize_domain function as written
expectations.evidence: Line 3 and line 34 explicitly say the single-function plan does not fit and should be replaced.

expectations.text: Grounds the advice in the appsmith-v2 principles catalogue and cites specific principle numbers
expectations.evidence: Cites P16, P19, P21, P15, P17, P2, P6, P20, P5, P8, P37 and quotes the contract throughout.

expectations.text: Produces a structured conformance review report with a clear overall verdict
expectations.evidence: Titled 'Conformance Report' with 'Verdict: Non-conformant'; follows the SKILL.md template (Deviations / Conformant / Not applicable / Summary).

expectations.text: Identifies the relevant component(s): Backend HTTP Routes and the service/database layer
expectations.evidence: Lists 'Component(s) reviewed': Backend HTTP Routes, Backend Services, Database Modules & Models, Celery & Background Workers.

expectations.text: Flags that business logic lives in the route handler, violating the thin-edges standard (Principle 2)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 2 [P2] Thin edges, thick core: format validation, quota computation, record creation, and generation are all inline in the handler.

expectations.text: Flags that the route reaches directly into the database and models instead of delegating to a service (Principle 1)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 1 [P1]: the route imports get_session and the Export model, runs select() queries, and reaches past the service layer into database/ and models/.

expectations.text: Flags the missing typed Pydantic request and response schemas (Principle 7)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 3 [P7]: bare str args bind as query params and responses are untyped dicts; recommends Pydantic request/response models.

expectations.text: Flags that the slow export generation runs inline in the request path instead of on a queue (Principle 19)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 7 [P19]: write_export_file runs synchronously in the handler; recommends enqueueing a Celery task and returning 202.

expectations.text: Flags that creating an export is not idempotent, so a retried POST creates duplicate records (Principle 15)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 8 [P15] and 11: no idempotency key; a retried request creates a second export row; recommends create-or-return-existing semantics.

expectations.text: Gives a concrete, specific remediation for each flagged deviation and assigns a severity to it
expectations.evidence: Every deviation entry has an explicit Severity (blocking/should-fix/minor) and a concrete Remediation.

expectations.text: Cites the appsmith-v2 principle numbers behind each finding rather than only generic commentary
expectations.evidence: Every finding is tagged with its principle number ([P1], [P2], [P7], [P19], [P15], plus P28, P20, P9, P5, P17, P8, P12).

expectations.text: Flags that the SEO agent calling the new agent directly violates hub-and-spoke orchestration (Principle 38)
expectations.evidence: 'Problem 1' quotes Principle 38 and Principle 4; states the SEO agent calling SitemapAgent makes a spoke coordinate another spoke.

expectations.text: Recommends coordination flow through the orchestrator rather than direct agent-to-agent calls (Principle 4)
expectations.evidence: Line 17: the determination flows back to the orchestrator, which decides to dispatch; recommended shape step 4 forbids spoke-to-spoke calls.

expectations.text: Flags that deterministic sitemap-building should not live in an agent reasoning loop and belongs in a service or tool (Principles 2 and 6)
expectations.evidence: 'Problem 2': deterministic crawl + XML belongs in a service (thick core, P2) with a thin tool (P6/P7); 'the reasoning loop is the part to drop.'

expectations.text: Raises skill-first extension — a skill, tool, or service should be considered before a new agent (Principle 41)
expectations.evidence: Problem 2 quotes Principle 41 verbatim and applies the lowest-power-layer ladder; recommended shape step 5 adds a skill only if real instructional nuance exists.

expectations.text: Concludes that the proposed shape is off and gives a concrete recommended alternative shape
expectations.evidence: Concludes the shape cuts against how the Kite agentic system is built and provides a numbered 'Recommended shape'.

expectations.text: Grounds the critique in the appsmith-v2 principles catalogue and cites specific principle numbers
expectations.evidence: Cites P38, P4, P41, P6, P2, P9, P39, P34, P37 and quotes the catalogue.

expectations.text: Identifies the work as belonging to the Deployment & Domains area of the platform
expectations.evidence: Discusses custom-domain finalization but never locates it in the platform's component structure; gives generic 'Rails/Django/Node service' advice.

expectations.text: Recommends modeling the flow as a resumable state machine that commits each transition (Principle 16), not one blocking function
expectations.evidence: Lines 36-37: 'Model finalization as distinct steps driven by background jobs and the domain's status column'; recommends guarded state transitions.

expectations.text: Recommends the domain move through explicit persisted intermediate states that can resume after a crash
expectations.evidence: Recommends statuses pending -> dns_pending -> dns_verified -> gsc_pending -> active and 'Persist status transitions, not just the final value' (line 63).

expectations.text: Recommends running the slow external GSC verification work asynchronously off the request path (Principle 19)
expectations.evidence: Lines 48-49: 'let the queue hold the wait, not a thread'; enqueue jobs rather than block.

expectations.text: Recommends bounding the verification retries with backoff and an explicit terminal state (Principle 21)
expectations.evidence: Line 61: 'Bounded retries + a terminal failure state' with a max attempt count.

expectations.text: Explicitly advises against the user's proposed single blocking finalize_domain function as written
expectations.evidence: Line 3 'the part I'd push back on'; line 80 'don't let it be a synchronous function that blocks on Google.'

expectations.text: Grounds the advice in the appsmith-v2 principles catalogue and cites specific principle numbers
expectations.evidence: No principle numbers; explicitly generic — 'I don't have your actual source', 'Rails/Django/Node service, etc.'

expectations.text: Produces a structured conformance review report with a clear overall verdict
expectations.evidence: Structured review grouped into Blocking / Should fix / Minor with a Summary stating 'I can't approve it as-is.'

expectations.text: Identifies the relevant component(s): Backend HTTP Routes and the service/database layer
expectations.evidence: Reviews the file directly; does not identify named platform components or a service/database layer.

expectations.text: Flags that business logic lives in the route handler, violating the thin-edges standard (Principle 2)
expectations.evidence: Flags the synchronous generation and quota inefficiency but does not frame business-logic-in-the-route as a thin-edges violation or recommend extracting a service.

expectations.text: Flags that the route reaches directly into the database and models instead of delegating to a service (Principle 1)
expectations.evidence: Flags the DB session handling but accepts the route running raw select() queries; does not flag the missing service/db layer or the layering violation.

expectations.text: Flags the missing typed Pydantic request and response schemas (Principle 7)
expectations.evidence: Minor section: 'No response model ... Add request/response models for validation'; suggests an Enum for export_format.

expectations.text: Flags that the slow export generation runs inline in the request path instead of on a queue (Principle 19)
expectations.evidence: Blocking issue 3: 'The export is generated synchronously inside the request'; recommends enqueueing a job and returning 202.

expectations.text: Flags that creating an export is not idempotent, so a retried POST creates duplicate records (Principle 15)
expectations.evidence: Flags a quota race condition and stranded pending rows, but does not flag the create itself as non-idempotent or that a retry creates a duplicate.

expectations.text: Gives a concrete, specific remediation for each flagged deviation and assigns a severity to it
expectations.evidence: Each issue has a concrete fix with code and is grouped under an explicit severity heading.

expectations.text: Cites the appsmith-v2 principle numbers behind each finding rather than only generic commentary
expectations.evidence: No principle numbers; advice is generic ('Most FastAPI codebases...', 'Check how other route files...').

expectations.text: Flags that the SEO agent calling the new agent directly violates hub-and-spoke orchestration (Principle 38)
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'SEO agent calling SitemapAgent directly — avoid this' explains hidden control flow and an NxN coupling mesh vs a star.

expectations.text: Recommends coordination flow through the orchestrator rather than direct agent-to-agent calls (Principle 4)
expectations.evidence: Line 14: the SEO agent should 'signal a need' and 'let the orchestrator decide whether to dispatch the sitemap work.'

expectations.text: Flags that deterministic sitemap-building should not live in an agent reasoning loop and belongs in a service or tool (Principles 2 and 6)
expectations.evidence: Section 2: crawling + XML formatting is 'deterministic mechanical work'; recommends a deterministic tool with 'no model in the hot path.'

expectations.text: Raises skill-first extension — a skill, tool, or service should be considered before a new agent (Principle 41)
expectations.evidence: Weighs tool vs agent but never raises a skill as the lowest-power option or the 'can a skill do this?' framing; no skill-first concept.

expectations.text: Concludes that the proposed shape is off and gives a concrete recommended alternative shape
expectations.evidence: Provides a 'Recommended shape' diagram and a 'Bottom line' concluding the packaging is off.

expectations.text: Grounds the critique in the appsmith-v2 principles catalogue and cites specific principle numbers
expectations.evidence: No principle numbers; generic reasoning ('agents reason; tools do').
